label: Human evaluation & preference studies

node.label: Human evaluation & preference studies
node.definition: Human evaluation measures quality, preference, usefulness, risk, or experience through controlled studies, rubrics, pairwise comparisons, and expert review.
node.why_it_matters: The role of Human evaluation & preference studies is to connect Statistics & experimental design, Preference learning to downstream capabilities including LLM-as-judge & automated critique, Fairness, bias & subgroup analysis, Safety policies, constitutional constraints & alignment.
examples: substrate for Fairness, bias & subgroup analysis
substrate for Safety policies, constitutional constraints & alignment
limitations: Coverage is necessarily incomplete: measured proxies, policies, and test suites cannot enumerate every deployment context or adversary. Its direct dependencies include Statistics & experimental design, Preference learning.
